survey.name: Survey
survey.res: Please select one from the dropdown menu
You have left question(s) unanswered. Please provide an answer.
You have left question(s) unanswered. Please provide an answer.
You have left some questions unanswered. You will find the items that need you attention shown below.

res.label: PROMPTERROR
res.strong: You have left question(s) unanswered. Are you sure you do not have an opinion? If not, you can skip to the next question.

res.label: PROMPTERROR1
res.strong: You have left question(s) unanswered. Are you sure you do not have an opinion? If not, you can skip to the next question.

res.label: PROMPTERROR2
res.strong: You have left question(s) unanswered. Are you sure you do not have an opinion? If not, you can skip to the next question.

exit.b: Thank you for completing the survey!

html.label: Start_Info
html.p:
Thanks for taking part. This survey helps us understand how people think about health care and public policy today. There are no right or wrong answers — we’re interested in your point of view.
Let’s begin.

survey.html:
<style>.nextPage, .button { display: none !important; }</style>
Your responses are held strictly confidential. See our Privacy Policy and commitment to research ethics and integrity.
Thanks for taking part. This survey helps us understand how people think about health care and public policy today. There are no right or wrong answers — we’re interested in your point of view.
Let’s begin.
<button id="sc-start-btn" type="button">READY, SET, GO!</button>
1 of 6 Questions
Are you registered to vote?
✓ YES
✕ NO
Not Sure
2 of 6 Questions
Please enter your 5-digit Zip Code
(the Zip Code of your primary residence where you are registered to vote)
<input class="zip-digit" id="sc-zip-1" type="tel" maxlength="1" inputmode="numeric" autocomplete="off" aria-label="ZIP digit 1"> <input class="zip-digit" id="sc-zip-2" type="tel" maxlength="1" inputmode="numeric" autocomplete="off" aria-label="ZIP digit 2"> <input class="zip-digit" id="sc-zip-3" type="tel" maxlength="1" inputmode="numeric" autocomplete="off" aria-label="ZIP digit 3"> <input class="zip-digit" id="sc-zip-4" type="tel" maxlength="1" inputmode="numeric" autocomplete="off" aria-label="ZIP digit 4"> <input class="zip-digit" id="sc-zip-5" type="tel" maxlength="1" inputmode="numeric" autocomplete="off" aria-label="ZIP digit 5">
<button class="zip-why-trigger" type="button"> ?
WHY DO WE ASK THIS?
</button>
Your ZIP code helps us ensure we’re hearing from people across different parts of the country, building a representative sample. FUN FACT: ZIP codes tie you to your congressional district, so we can ask follow‑up questions about your community — they never reveal your name or exact address.
<label class="sc-pna-row"> <input class="sc-pna-chk" id="zip-pna" type="checkbox"> Prefer not to answer </label>
3 of 6 Questions
Tell us how old you are.
Enter two digits — e.g., “4” then “5” for age 45
<input class="age-digit" id="sc-age-1" type="tel" maxlength="1" inputmode="numeric" autocomplete="off" aria-label="Age tens digit"> <input class="age-digit" id="sc-age-2" type="tel" maxlength="1" inputmode="numeric" autocomplete="off" aria-label="Age units digit">
<label class="sc-pna-row"> <input class="sc-pna-chk" id="age-pna" type="checkbox"> Prefer not to respond </label>
4 of 6 Questions
What is your gender?
MALE
FEMALE
OTHER
<label class="sc-pna-row"> <input class="sc-pna-chk" id="gender-pna" type="checkbox"> Prefer not to respond </label>
5 of 6 Questions
Who did you vote for in the latest (2024) Presidential election?
Donald J Trump (R)
Kamala Harris (D)
Another Candidate
I didn’t have the chance to vote in this election
<label class="sc-pna-row"> <input class="sc-pna-chk" id="vote-pna" type="checkbox"> Prefer not to respond </label>
6 of 6 Questions
In politics today, do you consider yourself a…?
Republican
Democrat
IND Independent/
Other
<label class="sc-pna-row" id="party-pna-main"> <input class="sc-pna-chk" id="party-pna" type="checkbox"> Prefer not to respond </label>
Would you say you are a strong or not-so-strong Republican?
Strong
Not-so-strong
Republican
Democrat
<label class="sc-pna-row"> <input class="sc-pna-chk" id="party2-pna" type="checkbox"> Prefer not to respond </label>
Do you lean toward the…
Republicans
Democrats
NO I do not lean toward either
<label class="sc-pna-row"> <input class="sc-pna-chk" id="party3-pna" type="checkbox"> Prefer not to respond </label>
<button id="sc-continue-btn" type="button">CONTINUE ></button>

radio.label: QS1
radio.title: Are you registered to vote?

radio.label: QGENDER
radio.title: Which of the following best describes your gender?

radio.label: QVOTE24
radio.title: Who did you vote for in the latest (2024) Presidential election?
radio.row: I did not have the chance to vote

radio.label: QS5
radio.title: In politics today, do you consider yourself a…?

radio.label: QS5A
radio.title: Would you say you are a strong or not-so strong [pipe: QS5]?

radio.label: QS5B
radio.title: Do you lean towards the Republican or Democratic Party?

survey.term: Voted for someone else and is Independent

radio.label: XPARTY
radio.title: Hidden: Derived Party ID (1-7)

radio.label: TYPING_MODULE
radio.title: Hidden: Typing Module Assignment

html.label: QMD1_intro2
html.p: In this section we'll ask about a mix of political and social issues. Some questions may feel personal, and some statements you may agree with strongly and others not so much. Always just go with your first, honest reaction.
You'll see a series of cards, each with four / five statements. On each card, choose the one that sounds most like you and the one that sounds least like you.
Some statements may show up again—that's intentional. There are no right or wrong answers. Go with the statement that feels most true—and most familiar—to you.

block.label: b11

number.label: QMD1_Version
number.title: QMD1 - MaxDiff Version

block.note: DEM MaxDiff Loop: 10 items, 4 per task, 10 tasks

radio.label: QDEM_[loopvar: task]
radio.title: Select the one statement that sounds MOST like your own point of view (on the left), and the statement that sounds LEAST like you (on the right)
radio.exec: setupMaxDiffItemsI( QMD1_md, "v%d_t%d" % (QMD1_Version.val, [loopvar: task]), QDEM_[loopvar: task]) p.MDcount = str(QMD1_md_loop_expanded.order.index([loopvar: task]-1)+1)
radio.row: TTech and data can improve care, but only if we stop bias, protect privacy, and provide benefits fairly.
II trust mainstream experts and prefer step-by-step fixes—strengthening the ACA, not ripping it up.
Digital tools, AI, and start-ups can universalize care faster than big bureaucracies—let’s remove red tape.
Corporate greed hurts ordinary people. Government must crack down on prices, ads, and junk food.
Healthcare is a human right. We need Medicare-for-All or something very close to it.
Unions fought for good coverage. Policy should protect employer benefits and bargaining power.
Racism and inequality drive poor health. Equity must be built into every health decision and budget.
Reproductive freedom—including abortion access—is non-negotiable and central to health policy.
MMy faith calls me to fight for the poor and for broader access—especially in under-served communities.
Climate change is a public-health emergency. Clean energy and environmental justice are health policy.

text.label: QMD1
text.title: Hidden: DEM MaxDiff B-W Matrix
text.exec: p.sum=[] groupSize = 4 groups = [p.maxdiff1[i:i+groupSize] for i in range(0, len(p.maxdiff1), groupSize)] for col in range(0, 10): rowsForColumn = groups[col] for r in rowsForColumn: rowNum = int(r)-1 QMD1.rows[rowNum][col].val=0 for i in range(0,10): best = "QDEM_{}".format(i+1) worst = "QDEM_{}".format(i+1) best1=allQuestions[best].best.val worst1=allQuestions[worst].worst.val QMD1.rows[best1][i].val=1 QMD1.rows[worst1][i].val=-1 for i in QMD1.rows: a=0 for j in range(10): if QMD1[i][j].val!=None: a1=QMD1[i][j].val a+=int(a1) QMD1[i].c11.val=a

text.label: XDEM_SEG
text.title: HIDDEN: XDEM_SEG (Z-Scores)
text.exec:
DEM_U = [-0.60198, -0.31771, -1.69065, 0.128199, 1.596402, 0.095769, 0.370408, 0.965797, -1.03699, 0.490752] DEM_S = [1.590145, 1.904432, 1.809285, 1.851639, 1.958779, 1.680348, 1.76441, 1.862097, 2.16036, 1.804034] DEM_MAX_F = []
for i in range(10): XDEM_SEG.rows[i].c1.val=QMD1.rows[i].c11.val DEM_MAX_F.append(float(QMD1.rows[i].c11.val)) for i in range(10): XDEM_SEG.rows[i].c2.val = DEM_U[i] for i in range(10): XDEM_SEG.rows[i].c3.val = DEM_S[i] for i in range(10): XDEM_SEG.rows[i].c4.val = (DEM_MAX_F[i] - DEM_U[i])/DEM_S[i]
JUSTICE_U = 4.868184 JUSTICE_S = 1.515216 INDUSTRY_U = 5.495869 INDUSTRY_S = 1.315719
justice_raw = float(QMD4.r1.val) if QMD4.r1.val else 4.0 industry_raw = float(QMD4.r2.val) if QMD4.r2.val else 4.0
XDEM_SEG.r13.c1.val = justice_raw XDEM_SEG.r13.c2.val = JUSTICE_U XDEM_SEG.r13.c3.val = JUSTICE_S XDEM_SEG.r13.c4.val = (justice_raw - JUSTICE_U) / JUSTICE_S
XDEM_SEG.r14.c1.val = industry_raw XDEM_SEG.r14.c2.val = INDUSTRY_U XDEM_SEG.r14.c3.val = INDUSTRY_S XDEM_SEG.r14.c4.val = (industry_raw - INDUSTRY_U) / INDUSTRY_S
text.row: Tech and data can improve care, but only if we stop bias, protect privacy, and provide benefits fairly.
I trust mainstream experts and prefer step-by-step fixes—strengthening the ACA, not ripping it up.
Digital tools, AI, and start-ups can universalize care faster than big bureaucracies—let’s remove red tape.
Corporate greed hurts ordinary people. Government must crack down on prices, ads, and junk food.
Healthcare is a human right. We need Medicare-for-All or something very close to it.
Unions fought for good coverage. Policy should protect employer benefits and bargaining power.
Racism and inequality drive poor health. Equity must be built into every health decision and budget.
Reproductive freedom—including abortion access—is non-negotiable and central to health policy.
My faith calls me to fight for the poor and for broader access—especially in under-served communities.
Climate change is a public-health emergency. Clean energy and environmental justice are health policy.

block.exec:
import math
def centroidDistance(resp_answers, segment_coeff): return sum((a - c)**2 for a, c in zip(resp_answers, segment_coeff))
def centroidAllSegments(answers, centroid_matrix): num_segments = len(centroid_matrix[0]) d2_list = [] for c in range(num_segments): segment_col = [row[c] for row in centroid_matrix] d2_list.append(centroidDistance(answers, segment_col)) return d2_list
p.dem_z = [float(XDEM_SEG.rows[i].c4.val) for i in range(10)] p.dem_z.append(float(XDEM_SEG.r13.c4.val)) p.dem_z.append(float(XDEM_SEG.r14.c4.val))
dem_centroids = [ [-0.5135, -0.1429, -0.3291, 0.544, 0.9293, -0.3204], [-0.039, -0.8206, -0.544, 0.1583, 0.6292, 0.8164], [-0.4356, -0.3798, -0.3126, 1.1914, 0.6485, -0.4382], [0.4891, -0.5913, 0.7544, 0.2422, -0.1966, -0.7195], [0.5194, 0.2926, 0.2316, 0.1168, -1.3929, 0.1419], [-0.1269, -0.1611, 0.5696, -0.2886, -0.2956, 0.2223], [0.257, 0.8708, -0.2005, -0.5704, -0.3712, -0.1654], [0.4032, -0.1316, 0.0902, -0.9386, -0.1134, 0.547], [-0.7075, 1.0908, -0.1312, 0.2406, 0.0411, -0.5795], [0.1626, -0.2097, -0.1184, -0.7095, 0.2866, 0.5285], [0.7218, 0.4355, -0.4757, -0.3332, -0.4753, 0.2212], [0.7961, 0.1727, 0.0062, -0.267, -0.5004, -0.0547] ]
p.dem_d2 = centroidAllSegments(p.dem_z, dem_centroids)
p.dem_exp = [math.exp(-d/2.0) for d in p.dem_d2] dem_exp_sum = sum(p.dem_exp) p.dem_prob = [(e/dem_exp_sum)*100.0 for e in p.dem_exp]
for i in range(6): XDEM_SOFTMAX.rows[i].c1.val = round(p.dem_d2[i], 4) XDEM_SOFTMAX.rows[i].c2.val = round(p.dem_exp[i], 6) XDEM_SOFTMAX.rows[i].c3.val = round(p.dem_prob[i], 2)
sorted_probs = sorted(enumerate(p.dem_prob), key=lambda x: -x[1]) XDEM_SEG_FINAL_1.val = XDEM_SEG_FINAL_1.rows[sorted_probs[0][0]].index XDEM_SEG_FINAL_2.val = XDEM_SEG_FINAL_2.rows[sorted_probs[1][0]].index
gap = sorted_probs[0][1] - sorted_probs[1][1] XDEM_CONFIDENCE.r1.val = round(sorted_probs[0][1], 2) XDEM_CONFIDENCE.r2.val = round(sorted_probs[1][1], 2) XDEM_CONFIDENCE.r3.val = round(gap, 2) entropy_val = -sum((pr/100.0)*math.log(pr/100.0+1e-12) for pr in p.dem_prob) XDEM_CONFIDENCE.r4.val = round(entropy_val, 4)

radio.label: XDEM_SEG_FINAL_1
radio.title: CENTROID+SOFTMAX - DEM SEGMENTS - First Maximum

radio.label: XDEM_SEG_FINAL_2
radio.title: CENTROID+SOFTMAX - DEM SEGMENTS - Second Maximum

block.label: b12

number.label: QMD3_Version
number.title: QMD3 - MaxDiff Version

block.note: GOP MaxDiff Loop: 12 items, 4 per task, 12 tasks

radio.label: QGOP_[loopvar: task]
radio.title: Select the one statement that sounds MOST like your own point of view (on the left), and the statement that sounds LEAST like you (on the right)
radio.exec: setupMaxDiffItemsI( QMD3_md, "v%d_t%d" % (QMD3_Version.val, [loopvar: task]), QGOP_[loopvar: task]) p.MDcount = str(QMD3_md_loop_expanded.order.index([loopvar: task]-1)+1)
radio.row: Government should stay small but defend marriage, family life, and personal responsibility.
Big pharma and big agribusiness can’t be trusted; local food and local control are healthier.
Drug and hospital prices are crushing people. I don’t care who fixes it—just bring costs down.
Small-town hospitals keep closing. Government must improve access to care for rural America.
Vaccines are unsafe, full stop. No one should be forced or pressured to take them.
MMy body, my choice—whether mandates, supplements, or experimental drugs. Government should butt out.
MModern medicine ignores the whole person. Natural and alternative therapies deserve equal respect and coverage.
Clean, whole-food living—not pills—keeps us healthy. The system ignores it because there’s no profit in it.
Human beings thrive on meat-heavy, low-carb diets. Government guidelines are junk science.
We must protect U.S. patients, workers, and supply chains first. Foreign competition and immigration come second.
Free markets work best, but I defer to FDA and CDC experts on safety and innovation.
Competition—private insurance, transparent prices, fewer rules—is the key to cheaper, better care.

text.label: QMD3
text.title: Hidden: GOP MaxDiff B-W Matrix
text.exec: p.sum=[] groupSize = 4 groups = [p.maxdiff1[i:i+groupSize] for i in range(0, len(p.maxdiff1), groupSize)] for col in range(0, 12): rowsForColumn = groups[col] for r in rowsForColumn: rowNum = int(r)-1 QMD3.rows[rowNum][col].val=0 for i in range(0,12): best = "QGOP_{}".format(i+1) worst = "QGOP_{}".format(i+1) best1=allQuestions[best].best.val worst1=allQuestions[worst].worst.val QMD3.rows[best1][i].val=1 QMD3.rows[worst1][i].val=-1 for i in QMD3.rows: a=0 for j in range(12): if QMD3[i][j].val!=None: a1=QMD3[i][j].val a+=int(a1) QMD3[i].c13.val=a
text.row: Government should stay small but defend marriage, family life, and personal responsibility.
Big pharma and big agribusiness can’t be trusted; local food and local control are healthier.
Drug and hospital prices are crushing people. I don’t care who fixes it—just bring costs down.
Small-town hospitals keep closing. Government must improve access to care for rural America.
Vaccines are unsafe, full stop. No one should be forced or pressured to take them.
My body, my choice—whether mandates, supplements, or experimental drugs. Government should butt out.
Modern medicine ignores the whole person. Natural and alternative therapies deserve equal respect and coverage.
Clean, whole-food living—not pills—keeps us healthy. The system ignores it because there’s no profit in it.
Human beings thrive on meat-heavy, low-carb diets. Government guidelines are junk science.
We must protect U.S. patients, workers, and supply chains first. Foreign competition and immigration come second.
Free markets work best, but I defer to FDA and CDC experts on safety and innovation.
Competition—private insurance, transparent prices, fewer rules—is the key to cheaper, better care.

text.label: XGOP_SEG
text.title: HIDDEN: XGOP_SEG (Z-Scores)
text.row: Government should stay small but defend marriage, family life, and personal responsibility.
Big pharma and big agribusiness can’t be trusted; local food and local control are healthier.
Drug and hospital prices are crushing people. I don’t care who fixes it—just bring costs down.
Small-town hospitals keep closing. Government must improve access to care for rural America.
Vaccines are unsafe, full stop. No one should be forced or pressured to take them.
My body, my choice—whether mandates, supplements, or experimental drugs. Government should butt out.
Modern medicine ignores the whole person. Natural and alternative therapies deserve equal respect and coverage.
Clean, whole-food living—not pills—keeps us healthy. The system ignores it because there’s no profit in it.
Human beings thrive on meat-heavy, low-carb diets. Government guidelines are junk science.
We must protect U.S. patients, workers, and supply chains first. Foreign competition and immigration come second.
Free markets work best, but I defer to FDA and CDC experts on safety and innovation.
Competition—private insurance, transparent prices, fewer rules—is the key to cheaper, better care.

block.exec:
import math
def centroidDistance(resp_answers, segment_coeff): return sum((a - c)**2 for a, c in zip(resp_answers, segment_coeff))
def centroidAllSegments(answers, centroid_matrix): num_segments = len(centroid_matrix[0]) d2_list = [] for c in range(num_segments): segment_col = [row[c] for row in centroid_matrix] d2_list.append(centroidDistance(answers, segment_col)) return d2_list
p.gop_z = [float(x) for x in XGOP_SEG.c4.values]
gop_centroids = [ [0.2, 0.0096, 0.9146, -0.1336, -0.6956, 0.5846, 0.4648, -0.2554, -0.739, -0.8787], [-0.0608, 0.0308, -0.5647, 0.0053, 0.7257, -0.0362, 0.0185, -0.4401, 0.1704, 0.6758], [-0.0633, 0.67, -0.4791, -0.3529, 0.712, -0.2058, -0.5104, -0.4564, 0.3953, 0.0561], [0.8912, 0.4863, -0.2283, 0.1686, 0.9888, -0.7418, -0.9072, 0.1943, -0.1989, -0.4581], [0.1561, -0.7521, -0.6867, 0.6036, -0.1513, -0.0754, 0.8258, 0.3333, -0.0793, 1.0337], [-0.6021, 0.1277, -0.2994, 0.0551, 0.2664, -0.4837, -0.1493, 0.1478, 0.5757, 0.4379], [-0.1778, -0.9908, -0.8135, 0.233, -0.1464, 0.0851, 0.236, 0.9657, 0.3877, 0.9297], [-0.7705, -0.2343, -0.3477, -1.0279, -0.572, 0.6932, 0.1564, 0.2632, 0.5845, 0.5142], [0.1964, -0.5763, 0.0446, 1.363, -0.0369, -0.4206, 0.9004, 0.2931, -0.5885, 0.1112], [-0.3176, 0.7097, 0.4794, -0.8491, -0.2762, 0.4546, -0.4476, -0.8232, 0.3467, -0.4787], [0.5922, 0.291, 1.1052, 0.6792, 0.0203, -0.5188, -0.4748, 0.0904, -0.7155, -1.192], [-0.0166, 0.3879, 0.9236, -0.8652, -0.687, 0.6888, -0.2467, -0.4652, -0.1536, -0.8748] ]
p.gop_d2 = centroidAllSegments(p.gop_z, gop_centroids)
p.gop_exp = [math.exp(-d/2.0) for d in p.gop_d2] gop_exp_sum = sum(p.gop_exp) p.gop_prob = [(e/gop_exp_sum)*100.0 for e in p.gop_exp]
for i in range(10): XGOP_SOFTMAX.rows[i].c1.val = round(p.gop_d2[i], 4) XGOP_SOFTMAX.rows[i].c2.val = round(p.gop_exp[i], 6) XGOP_SOFTMAX.rows[i].c3.val = round(p.gop_prob[i], 2)
sorted_probs = sorted(enumerate(p.gop_prob), key=lambda x: -x[1]) XGOP_SEG_FINAL_1.val = XGOP_SEG_FINAL_1.rows[sorted_probs[0][0]].index XGOP_SEG_FINAL_2.val = XGOP_SEG_FINAL_2.rows[sorted_probs[1][0]].index
gap = sorted_probs[0][1] - sorted_probs[1][1] XGOP_CONFIDENCE.r1.val = round(sorted_probs[0][1], 2) XGOP_CONFIDENCE.r2.val = round(sorted_probs[1][1], 2) XGOP_CONFIDENCE.r3.val = round(gap, 2) entropy_val = -sum((pr/100.0)*math.log(pr/100.0+1e-12) for pr in p.gop_prob) XGOP_CONFIDENCE.r4.val = round(entropy_val, 4)

radio.label: XGOP_SEG_FINAL_1
radio.title: CENTROID+SOFTMAX - GOP SEGMENTS - First Maximum

radio.label: XGOP_SEG_FINAL_2
radio.title: CENTROID+SOFTMAX - GOP SEGMENTS - Second Maximum

radio.label: XSEG_ASSIGNED
radio.title: Final Segment Assignment (1-16)

select.label: QMD4
select.title: Tell us how much you agree or disagree with these statements
select.comment: Tap where you fall on each statement...
select.row: The federal government should invest extra funds in communities of color that have suffered historic health injustices, even if it means higher taxes
Big corporations have too much influence over public health policies

survey.html: People shape decisions in different ways. The questions that follow help us understand how you tend to engage and where your influence shows up. At the end of the survey, we’ll share a snapshot of your influence profile.

checkbox.label: QMD6
checkbox.title: How have you made your voice heard?
checkbox.comment: Select all that apply. At the end, you'll see your Influence Compass.
checkbox.row: I've worked inside government, held office, or staffed those who do
I've helped decide who runs for office or who gets hired in political or government roles
I advise organizations, sit on boards, or have testified on policy matters
I've written, spoken publicly, or published on issues
I've organized people, worked campaigns, or rallied others to action
I vote, follow current events, and stay involved in my community

checkbox.label: QMD7
checkbox.title: Which of these have you done?
checkbox.comment: Select all that apply. At the end, you'll see your Influence Compass.
checkbox.row: Met (in person or video) with congressional, agency, or state legislative staff
Sat on the board or executive committee of any health, policy, or business association?
Worked with or advised a Washington-based organization (think tank, patient advocacy group, trade association)
Testified or submitted written comments to a state legislative or Congressional committee

checkbox.label: QMD88
checkbox.title: In the past 12 months, have you taken any of the following actions related to a public policy issue?

radio.label: QMD89
radio.title: Across all your public social-media accounts about how many followers or subscribers do you have in total?
radio.comment: Including from X/Twitter, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook pages, Substack, podcast, YouTube, etc

radio.label: QMD90
radio.title: How many times over the past 30 days have you…
radio.comment: Across any social media platform you use (Facebook, X, LinkedIn, TikTok, or others), Approximately how many times have you done any of the following to advocate on a public policy issue?

row.label: r1
row.b: Created an original post or video

radio.label: MAHA1
radio.title: How familiar are you with the term ‘MAHA’ (Make America Healthy Again)?
radio.row:
Aware, but not
sure what it
means

Heard of it, but
know nothing
about it

radio.label: MAHA2
radio.title: When you hear the term MAHA, what does it most closely represent to you?
radio.row: A movement focused on food quality, nutrition, and chronic disease
A broader critique of drug and food companies, and the health system
A political slogan more than a real movement
A personal health or lifestyle philosophy
A fringe or extreme health movement
The movement around Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.
The health movement led by Donald Trump
Something unclear or hard to define

select.label: MAHA3
select.title: Overall, what is your impression of the MAHA movement or idea?

radio.label: MAHA4
radio.title: Which best describes your relationship to the MAHA label?
radio.row: I am a member of the national organization
I agree with some of the ideas, but wouldn’t identify with it
I’ve heard of it, but don’t relate to it

survey.html:
We promise this is the last stretch.
Just a few background questions so we can see how different walks of life connect with different ways of thinking.

radio.label: ETHNIC
radio.title: Are you, yourself, of Hispanic or Latino background?

radio.label: RACE
radio.title: Which of the following best describes your race?

radio.label: VET
radio.title: Have you ever served on active duty in the U.S. Armed Forces, Reserves, or National Guard?

radio.label: UNION
radio.title: Are you currently, or have you ever been, part of a labor union?

radio.label: EDUCATION
radio.title: Please indicate the highest level of school you completed or the highest degree you have received…

select.label: REL
select.title: What is your present religion, if any?
select.choice: Orthodox, such as Greek or Russian Orthodox

radio.label: REL2
radio.title: Would you describe yourself a born-again or evangelical Christian, or not?

select.label: HHI
select.title: Last year – that is, in 2025 – what was your total family income from all sources, before taxes…

text.label: HHIXEMAIL
text.title: Please share your email address to see which PRISM segment you are most closely aligned with.

survey.term: This text will appear in the Response Summary

html.label: cm3
html.p: To help us better understand how people encounter and engage with information about health, this section asks about the media, platforms, and voices that shape your information environment.
These questions help us understand where you go for information, who you trust, and how different sources influence the way you think about health and healthcare.

checkbox.label: HHIX100
checkbox.title: Which of the following do you use to get health or political information? Select all that apply.
checkbox.comment: Include any platform you use at least occasionally — it doesn't have to be your main source.
checkbox.row:
Podcasts
Audio shows you stream or download

Facebook
Posts, groups, or pages on Facebook

X (Twitter)
Posts and threads on X

Instagram
Posts, reels, or stories on Instagram

Substack or email newsletters
Newsletters delivered by email

Online news sites and blogs
Websites, news sites, or blogs

Rumble or alternative video platforms
Rumble, Odysee, Brighteon, or similar

Telegram or Signal channels
Channels or groups on Telegram or Signal Cable or broadcast TV

row.label: r11
row.strong: Cable news or broadcast TV programs

checkbox.row:
Radio or talk radio
AM/FM radio, satellite radio, or talk radio shows

checkbox.label: HHIX101
checkbox.title: Which of the following topics do you follow for health or political information? Tap on all that apply.
checkbox.comment: Think about the sources, podcasts, newsletters, or social accounts you follow regularly.
checkbox.row:
Health policy
(Insurance, drug prices, Medical reform)

radio.label: HHIX102
radio.title: When was the last time you used this source for news and information on this topic?
radio.comment: if you’ve never heard of it before, don’t worry – you’re definitely not alone. The next source slides in as soon as you answer. No time to waste - keep swiping!

radio.label: HHIX103
radio.title: Which publication or site should we have asked about?

checkbox.label: HHIX104
checkbox.title: Which of the following voices or creators do you follow on this topic?
checkbox.comment: Scan the list and tap on any that you know and follow, no matter how often

textarea.label: HHIX105
textarea.title: Which voices or creators did we forget to include?
